race.name: Niseko Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through Hokkaido's powder capital — where the world's deepest snowpack meets Japanese precision on trails beneath Mount Yōtei's volcanic cone.

vitals.aid_stations: 5 feed stations with onigiri, miso soup, and Japanese sweets

climate.description: Niseko receives some of the world's heaviest snowfall — over 15 meters per season. The Sea of Japan effect creates continuous powder dumps from November through April. Temperatures range from -15°C to -2°C. Snow is so reliable it's essentially guaranteed.
challenges: Extremely heavy snowfall can bury the course overnight
Low visibility during frequent snow squalls
Deep powder adjacent to groomed tracks makes recovery from falls difficult
Course preparation is a constant battle against fresh snowfall

course.primary: A 42km course through the Niseko valley beneath Mount Yōtei's perfect volcanic cone. The route passes through birch forest and open farmland with the iconic Yōtei-zan backdrop. Japan's deepest snowpack means the course sits atop meters of compacted snow. Impeccable Japanese grooming standards.
course.surface: Meticulously groomed classic tracks — Japanese precision standard
course.grooming: Outstanding — Japanese attention to detail applied to grooming
features: Mount Yōtei volcanic cone panorama — Japan's 'Little Fuji'
World-class powder snow region — 15+ meters annual snowfall
Hokkaido birch forest and open valley skiing
Growing international participation from Niseko's expat community

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum snow reliability and grooming quality — Niseko's world-famous powder and Japanese precision deliver the best conditions in Asia. Maximum organization score reflects Japan's legendary event management. Low altitude is offset by the extreme maritime snowfall.

history.summary: Founded in 1982 as Hokkaido's premier citizen XC ski marathon, the Niseko Ski Marathon leverages the region's legendary snowfall and Mount Yōtei's stunning volcanic scenery. Niseko has become internationally famous for powder skiing since the 2000s, and the XC marathon benefits from the area's growing international winter sports community. Japan has a deep XC skiing tradition in Hokkaido.
notable_facts: Niseko receives over 15 meters of snowfall annually — among the deepest in the world
Mount Yōtei (1898m) is called 'Ezo Fuji' for its resemblance to Mount Fuji
Hokkaido has hosted XC ski events since the 1930s
Niseko's international community has grown the event's non-Japanese participation
Japan's XC ski marathon culture is well-established with over a dozen annual events

videos.title: American Newell wins Sprint at 2013 Winter Games - Universal Sports
videos.description: http://www.UniversalSports.com 2013, Snow Farm, New Zealand, Winter Games, FIS Cross Country Skiing, Men's Sprint, American Skier Andrew Newell won with a final time of 2:52.96, beating out 2nd place skier Alexey Petukhov (RUS) by 2.07 seconds. Newell was 9th in the team sprint at the 2010 Winter Olympics. (Watch the full event at UniversalSports.com)

videos.title: Most insane ski line EVER
videos.description:
Watch Days Of My Youth free in the Matchstick Vault: https://matchstickpro.com/days-of-my-youth/
iTunes: http://geni.us/3iuE
Cody Townsend takes big mountain skiing to an entirely new level, straight-lining one of the gnarliest vertical chutes deep in the Alaska Tordrillos Mountain Range, earning himself the "Best Line" award at the 15th annual Powder Awards, along with "Best Male Performance" and "Full Throttle" titles as well.

"DAYS OF MY YOUTH" is now available at http://www.matchstickpro.com and http://www.RedBull.com/DaysOfMyYouth 

Red Bull Media House, in association with Matchstick Productions, present “DAYS OF MY YOUTH”, a new film that examines every skier’s lifelong affinity for the sport. Narrated to the prolific readings of Alan Watts, this film exposes the many joys and occasional struggles associated with a lifestyle built around skiing. Filmed over two years using state of the art techniques and technologies, this movie offers a unique glimpse into the journey of self-discovery that every skier experiences.  

Subscribe: http://bit.ly/MSPfilmsYT

More info at http://www.matchstickpro.com/

Follow us on
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MSPFilms
Instagram: http://instagram.com/mspfilms
Twitter: https://twitter.com/mspfilms

videos.title: The Race of The Century, Niseko 2015
videos.description: Trailer of Afzal and Patrick skiing on Family Run, NIseko

quotes.text: The weather is so good it's cloudy, the temperature is minus 2.2 degrees Celsius, perfect conditions for skiers to enjoy their strenuous race. We had about 2,000 participants from 16 countries this year.
The 50 kilometer event with a course set in the world renowned beautiful wilderness of Sapporo will give satisfaction to both full-fledged competitors and enthusiasts.
